azure_blob_storage_account_key.description: The Azure Blob Storage account key. If you set this value, you must not set the "Shared Access Signature", "Azure Tenant ID", "Azure Client ID", or "Azure Client Secret" fields.
azure_blob_storage_account_key.title: Azure Blob Storage Account Key

azure_blob_storage_account_name.description: The name of the Azure Blob Storage Account. Read more here.
azure_blob_storage_account_name.title: Azure Blob Storage Account Name

azure_blob_storage_container_name.description: The name of the Azure Blob Storage Container. Read more here.
azure_blob_storage_container_name.title: Azure Blob Storage Container Name

azure_blob_storage_endpoint_domain_name.description: This is Azure Blob Storage endpoint domain name. Leave default value (or leave it empty if run container from command line) to use Microsoft native from example.
azure_blob_storage_endpoint_domain_name.title: Azure Blob Storage Endpoint Domain Name

azure_blob_storage_spill_size.description: The amount of megabytes after which the connector should spill the records in a new blob object. Make sure to configure size greater than individual records. Enter 0 if not applicable.
azure_blob_storage_spill_size.title: Azure Blob Storage Target Blob Size (MB)

azure_client_id.description: The Azure Active Directory (Entra ID) client ID. Required for Entra ID authentication.
azure_client_id.title: Azure Client ID

azure_client_secret.description: The Azure Active Directory (Entra ID) client secret. Required for Entra ID authentication.
azure_client_secret.title: Azure Client Secret

azure_tenant_id.description: The Azure Active Directory (Entra ID) tenant ID. Required for Entra ID authentication.
azure_tenant_id.title: Azure Tenant ID

shared_access_signature.description: A shared access signature (SAS) provides secure delegated access to resources in your storage account. Read more here. If you set this value, you must not set the "Azure Blob Storage Account Key", "Azure Tenant ID", "Azure Client ID", or "Azure Client Secret" fields.
shared_access_signature.title: Shared Access Signature

cdc_deletion_mode.description: Whether to execute CDC deletions as hard deletes (i.e. propagate source deletions to the destination), or soft deletes (i.e. leave a tombstone record in the destination). Defaults to hard deletes.
cdc_deletion_mode.title: CDC deletion mode

credentials_json.description: The contents of the JSON service account key. Check out the docs if you need help generating this key. Default credentials will be used if this field is left empty.
credentials_json.title: Service Account Key JSON (Required for cloud, optional for open-source)

dataset_id.description: The default BigQuery Dataset ID that tables are replicated to if the source does not specify a namespace. Read more here.
dataset_id.title: Default Dataset ID

dataset_location.description: The location of the dataset. Warning: Changes made after creation will not be applied. Read more here.
dataset_location.title: Dataset Location

disable_type_dedupe.description: Write the legacy "raw tables" format, to enable backwards compatibility with older versions of this connector.
disable_type_dedupe.title: Legacy raw tables

loading_method.description: The way data will be uploaded to BigQuery.

oneOf.description: Direct loading using batched SQL INSERT statements. This method uses the BigQuery driver to convert large INSERT statements into file uploads automatically.
oneOf.title: Batched Standard Inserts

oneOf.description: Writes large batches of records to a file, uploads the file to GCS, then uses COPY INTO to load your data into BigQuery.

credential.description: An HMAC key is a type of credential and can be associated with a service account or a user account in Cloud Storage. Read more here.

hmac_key_access_id.description: HMAC key access ID. When linked to a service account, this ID is 61 characters long; when linked to a user account, it is 24 characters long.
hmac_key_access_id.title: HMAC Access Key

hmac_key_secret.description: The corresponding secret for the access ID. It is a 40-character base-64 encoded string.
hmac_key_secret.title: HMAC Secret

credential.title: Credential

gcs_bucket_name.description: The name of the GCS bucket. Read more here.
gcs_bucket_name.title: GCS Bucket Name

gcs_bucket_path.description: Directory under the GCS bucket where data will be written.
gcs_bucket_path.title: GCS Bucket Path

keep_files_in_gcs-bucket.default: Delete all tmp files from GCS
keep_files_in_gcs-bucket.description: This upload method is supposed to temporary store records in GCS bucket. By this select you can chose if these records should be removed from GCS when migration has finished. The default "Delete all tmp files from GCS" value is used if not set explicitly.
enum: Delete all tmp files from GCS
Keep all tmp files in GCS
keep_files_in_gcs-bucket.title: GCS Tmp Files Post-Processing

oneOf.title: GCS Staging

loading_method.title: Loading Method

project_id.description: The GCP project ID for the project containing the target BigQuery dataset. Read more here.
project_id.title: Project ID

raw_data_dataset.description: Airbyte will use this dataset for various internal tables. In legacy raw tables mode, the raw tables will be stored in this dataset. Defaults to "airbyte_internal".
raw_data_dataset.title: Airbyte Internal Table Dataset Name

enable_json.description: Use the JSON type for Object fields. If disabled, the JSON will be converted to a string.
enable_json.title: Enable JSON

port.description: HTTP port of the database. Default(s) HTTP: 8123 — HTTPS: 8443
port.title: Port

protocol.description: Protocol for the database connection string.
protocol.title: Protocol

record_window_size.description: Warning: Tuning this parameter can impact the performances. The maximum number of records that should be written to a batch. The batch size limit is still limited to 70 Mb
record_window_size.title: Record Window Size

tunnel_method.description: Whether to initiate an SSH tunnel before connecting to the database, and if so, which kind of authentication to use.

oneOf.description: No ssh tunnel needed to connect to database
oneOf.title: No Tunnel

oneOf.description: Connect through a jump server tunnel host using username and ssh key

ssh_key.description: OS-level user account ssh key credentials in RSA PEM format ( created with ssh-keygen -t rsa -m PEM -f myuser_rsa )
ssh_key.title: SSH Private Key

tunnel_host.description: Hostname of the jump server host that allows inbound ssh tunnel.
tunnel_host.title: SSH Tunnel Jump Server Host

tunnel_port.description: Port on the proxy/jump server that accepts inbound ssh connections.
tunnel_port.title: SSH Connection Port

tunnel_user.description: OS-level username for logging into the jump server host
tunnel_user.title: SSH Login Username

oneOf.title: SSH Key Authentication

oneOf.description: Connect through a jump server tunnel host using username and password authentication

tunnel_host.description: Hostname of the jump server host that allows inbound ssh tunnel.
tunnel_host.title: SSH Tunnel Jump Server Host

tunnel_port.description: Port on the proxy/jump server that accepts inbound ssh connections.
tunnel_port.title: SSH Connection Port

tunnel_user.description: OS-level username for logging into the jump server host
tunnel_user.title: SSH Login Username

tunnel_user_password.description: OS-level password for logging into the jump server host
tunnel_user_password.title: Password

oneOf.title: Password Authentication

tunnel_method.title: SSH Tunnel Method

username.title: Username

credentials.description: Enter the site ID and API key to authenticate.
credentials.title: Credentials

access_key_id.description: The access key ID to access the S3 bucket. Airbyte requires Read and Write permissions to the given bucket. Read more here.
access_key_id.title: Access Key ID

bucket_path.description: All files in the bucket will be prefixed by this.
bucket_path.title: Prefix Path in the Bucket

role_arn.description: The ARN of the AWS role to assume. Only usable in Airbyte Cloud.
role_arn.title: Role ARN

secret_access_key.description: The corresponding secret to the access key ID. Read more here

accept_terms.description: You must agree to the Databricks JDBC Driver Terms & Conditions to use this connector.
accept_terms.title: Agree to the Databricks JDBC Driver Terms & Conditions

authentication.description: Authentication mechanism for Staging files and running queries
authentication.title: Authentication

database.description: The name of the unity catalog for the database
database.title: Databricks Unity Catalog Name

purge_staging_data.description: Default to 'true'. Switch it to 'false' for debugging purpose.
purge_staging_data.title: Purge Staging Files and Tables

raw_schema_override.description: The schema to write raw tables into (default: airbyte_internal)
raw_schema_override.title: Raw Table Schema Name

schema.description: The default schema tables are written. If not specified otherwise, the "default" will be used.
schema.title: Default Schema

credentials.description: Choose how to authenticate to HubSpot.

client_id.title: Client ID

client_secret.description: The client secret for your HubSpot developer application. See the Hubspot docs if you need help finding this secret.
client_secret.title: Client Secret

refresh_token.description: Refresh token to renew an expired access token. See the Hubspot docs if you need help finding this token.
refresh_token.title: Refresh Token

schema.description:
The configuration model for the Vector DB based destinations. This model is used to generate the UI for the destination configuration,
as well as to provide type safety for the configuration passed to the destination.

The configuration model is composed of four parts:
* Processing configuration
* Embedding configuration
* Indexing configuration
* Advanced configuration

Processing, embedding and advanced configuration are provided by this base class, while the indexing configuration is provided by the destination connector in the sub class.

oneOf.description: Use the OpenAI API to embed text. This option is using the text-embedding-ada-002 model with 1536 embedding dimensions.
oneOf.title: OpenAI

oneOf.description: Use the Cohere API to embed text.
oneOf.title: Cohere

oneOf.description: Use a fake embedding made out of random vectors with 1536 embedding dimensions. This is useful for testing the data pipeline without incurring any costs.
oneOf.title: Fake

oneOf.description: Use the Azure-hosted OpenAI API to embed text. This option is using the text-embedding-ada-002 model with 1536 embedding dimensions.

api_base.description: The base URL for your Azure OpenAI resource.  You can find this in the Azure portal under your Azure OpenAI resource
api_base.title: Resource base URL

deployment.description: The deployment for your Azure OpenAI resource.  You can find this in the Azure portal under your Azure OpenAI resource
deployment.title: Deployment

openai_key.description: The API key for your Azure OpenAI resource.  You can find this in the Azure portal under your Azure OpenAI resource
openai_key.title: Azure OpenAI API key

oneOf.title: Azure OpenAI

oneOf.description: Use a service that's compatible with the OpenAI API to embed text.

base_url.description: The base URL for your OpenAI-compatible service
base_url.title: Base URL

dimensions.description: The number of dimensions the embedding model is generating
dimensions.title: Embedding dimensions

model_name.description: The name of the model to use for embedding
model_name.title: Model name

oneOf.title: OpenAI-compatible

oneOf.description: Authenticate using an API token (suitable for Zilliz Cloud)

token.description: API Token for the Milvus instance
token.title: API Token

oneOf.title: API Token

oneOf.description: Authenticate using username and password (suitable for self-managed Milvus clusters)
oneOf.title: Username/Password

oneOf.description: Do not authenticate (suitable for locally running test clusters, do not use for clusters with public IP addresses)
oneOf.title: No auth

collection.description: The collection to load data into
collection.title: Collection Name

host.description: The public endpoint of the Milvus instance.
host.title: Public Endpoint

text_field.description: The field in the entity that contains the embedded text
text_field.title: Text Field

vector_field.description: The field in the entity that contains the vector
vector_field.title: Vector Field

omit_raw_text.description: Do not store the text that gets embedded along with the vector and the metadata in the destination. If set to true, only the vector and the metadata will be stored - in this case raw text for LLM use cases needs to be retrieved from another source.
omit_raw_text.title: Do not store raw text

chunk_overlap.description: Size of overlap between chunks in tokens to store in vector store to better capture relevant context
chunk_overlap.title: Chunk overlap

chunk_size.description: Size of chunks in tokens to store in vector store (make sure it is not too big for the context if your LLM)
chunk_size.title: Chunk size

field_name_mappings.description: List of fields to rename. Not applicable for nested fields, but can be used to rename fields already flattened via dot notation.

from_field.description: The field name in the source
from_field.title: From field name

to_field.description: The field name to use in the destination
to_field.title: To field name

field_name_mappings.title: Field name mappings

metadata_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be stored as metadata. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered metadata fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array. When specifying nested paths, all matching values are flattened into an array set to a field named by the path.
metadata_fields.title: Fields to store as metadata

text_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be used to calculate the embedding. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered text fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array.
text_fields.title: Text fields to embed

text_splitter.description: Split text fields into chunks based on the specified method.

oneOf.description: Split the text by the list of separators until the chunk size is reached, using the earlier mentioned separators where possible. This is useful for splitting text fields by paragraphs, sentences, words, etc.

keep_separator.description: Whether to keep the separator in the resulting chunks
keep_separator.title: Keep separator

separators.description: List of separator strings to split text fields by. The separator itself needs to be wrapped in double quotes, e.g. to split by the dot character, use ".". To split by a newline, use "\n".
separators.title: Separators

oneOf.title: By Separator

oneOf.description: Split the text by Markdown headers down to the specified header level. If the chunk size fits multiple sections, they will be combined into a single chunk.

split_level.description: Level of markdown headers to split text fields by. Headings down to the specified level will be used as split points
split_level.title: Split level

oneOf.title: By Markdown header

oneOf.description: Split the text by suitable delimiters based on the programming language. This is useful for splitting code into chunks.

language.description: Split code in suitable places based on the programming language
language.title: Language

oneOf.title: By Programming Language

text_splitter.title: Text splitter

schema.title: Destination Config

database.description: The name of the MSSQL database.
database.title: DB Name

host.description: The host name of the MSSQL database.
host.title: Host

jdbc_url_params.description: Additional properties to pass to the JDBC URL string when connecting to the database formatted as 'key=value' pairs separated by the symbol '&'. (example: key1=value1&key2=value2&key3=value3).
jdbc_url_params.title: JDBC URL Params

load_type.description: Specifies the type of load mechanism (e.g., BULK, INSERT) and its associated configuration.

oneOf.description: Configuration details for using the INSERT loading mechanism.
oneOf.title: Insert Load

oneOf.description: Configuration details for using the BULK loading mechanism.

azure_blob_storage_account_key.description: The Azure blob storage account key. Mutually exclusive with a Shared Access Signature
azure_blob_storage_account_key.title: Azure Blob Storage account key

azure_blob_storage_account_name.description: The name of the Azure Blob Storage account. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/storage/blobs/storage-blobs-introduction#storage-accounts
azure_blob_storage_account_name.title: Azure Blob Storage Account Name

azure_blob_storage_container_name.description: The name of the Azure Blob Storage container. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/storage/blobs/storage-blobs-introduction#containers
azure_blob_storage_container_name.title: Azure Blob Storage Container Name

bulk_load_data_source.description: Specifies the external data source name configured in MSSQL, which references the Azure Blob container. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/sql/t-sql/statements/bulk-insert-transact-sql
bulk_load_data_source.title: BULK Load Data Source

bulk_load_validate_values_pre_load.description: When enabled, Airbyte will validate all values before loading them into the destination table. This provides stronger data integrity guarantees but may significantly impact performance.
bulk_load_validate_values_pre_load.title: Pre-Load Value Validation

shared_access_signature.description: A shared access signature (SAS) provides secure delegated access to resources in your storage account. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/storage/common/storage-sas-overview.Mutually exclusive with an account key
shared_access_signature.title: Shared Access Signature

oneOf.title: Bulk Load

load_type.title: Load Type

password.description: The password associated with this username.
password.title: Password

port.description: The port of the MSSQL database.
port.title: Port

schema.description: The default schema tables are written to if the source does not specify a namespace. The usual value for this field is "public".
schema.title: Default Schema

ssl_method.description: The encryption method which is used to communicate with the database.

oneOf.description: The data transfer will not be encrypted.
oneOf.title: Unencrypted

oneOf.description: Use the certificate provided by the server without verification. (For testing purposes only!)
oneOf.title: Encrypted (trust server certificate)

oneOf.description: Verify and use the certificate provided by the server.

hostNameInCertificate.description: Specifies the host name of the server. The value of this property must match the subject property of the certificate.
hostNameInCertificate.title: Host Name In Certificate

trustStoreName.description: Specifies the name of the trust store.
trustStoreName.title: Trust Store Name

trustStorePassword.description: Specifies the password of the trust store.
trustStorePassword.title: Trust Store Password

oneOf.title: Encrypted (verify certificate)

ssl_method.title: SSL Method

tunnel_method.description: Whether to initiate an SSH tunnel before connecting to the database, and if so, which kind of authentication to use.

oneOf.description: No ssh tunnel needed to connect to database
oneOf.title: No Tunnel

oneOf.description: Connect through a jump server tunnel host using username and ssh key

ssh_key.description: OS-level user account ssh key credentials in RSA PEM format ( created with ssh-keygen -t rsa -m PEM -f myuser_rsa )
ssh_key.title: SSH Private Key

tunnel_host.description: Hostname of the jump server host that allows inbound ssh tunnel.
tunnel_host.title: SSH Tunnel Jump Server Host

tunnel_port.description: Port on the proxy/jump server that accepts inbound ssh connections.
tunnel_port.title: SSH Connection Port

tunnel_user.description: OS-level username for logging into the jump server host
tunnel_user.title: SSH Login Username

oneOf.title: SSH Key Authentication

oneOf.description: Connect through a jump server tunnel host using username and password authentication

tunnel_host.description: Hostname of the jump server host that allows inbound ssh tunnel.
tunnel_host.title: SSH Tunnel Jump Server Host

tunnel_port.description: Port on the proxy/jump server that accepts inbound ssh connections.
tunnel_port.title: SSH Connection Port

tunnel_user.description: OS-level username for logging into the jump server host
tunnel_user.title: SSH Login Username

tunnel_user_password.description: OS-level password for logging into the jump server host
tunnel_user_password.title: Password

user.title: User

indexing.description: Pinecone is a popular vector store that can be used to store and retrieve embeddings.

index.description: Pinecone index in your project to load data into
index.title: Index

pinecone_key.description: The Pinecone API key to use matching the environment (copy from Pinecone console)
pinecone_key.title: Pinecone API key

indexing.title: Indexing

omit_raw_text.description: Do not store the text that gets embedded along with the vector and the metadata in the destination. If set to true, only the vector and the metadata will be stored - in this case raw text for LLM use cases needs to be retrieved from another source.
omit_raw_text.title: Do not store raw text

chunk_overlap.description: Size of overlap between chunks in tokens to store in vector store to better capture relevant context
chunk_overlap.title: Chunk overlap

chunk_size.description: Size of chunks in tokens to store in vector store (make sure it is not too big for the context if your LLM)
chunk_size.title: Chunk size

field_name_mappings.description: List of fields to rename. Not applicable for nested fields, but can be used to rename fields already flattened via dot notation.

from_field.description: The field name in the source
from_field.title: From field name

to_field.description: The field name to use in the destination
to_field.title: To field name

field_name_mappings.title: Field name mappings

metadata_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be stored as metadata. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered metadata fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array. When specifying nested paths, all matching values are flattened into an array set to a field named by the path.
metadata_fields.title: Fields to store as metadata

text_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be used to calculate the embedding. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered text fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array.
text_fields.title: Text fields to embed

text_splitter.description: Split text fields into chunks based on the specified method.

oneOf.description: Split the text by the list of separators until the chunk size is reached, using the earlier mentioned separators where possible. This is useful for splitting text fields by paragraphs, sentences, words, etc.

keep_separator.description: Whether to keep the separator in the resulting chunks
keep_separator.title: Keep separator

separators.description: List of separator strings to split text fields by. The separator itself needs to be wrapped in double quotes, e.g. to split by the dot character, use ".". To split by a newline, use "\n".
separators.title: Separators

oneOf.title: By Separator

oneOf.description: Split the text by Markdown headers down to the specified header level. If the chunk size fits multiple sections, they will be combined into a single chunk.

split_level.description: Level of markdown headers to split text fields by. Headings down to the specified level will be used as split points
split_level.title: Split level

oneOf.title: By Markdown header

oneOf.description: Split the text by suitable delimiters based on the programming language. This is useful for splitting code into chunks.

language.description: Split code in suitable places based on the programming language
language.title: Language

oneOf.title: By Programming Language

text_splitter.title: Text splitter

schema.title: Destination Config

disable_type_dedupe.description: Disable Writing Final Tables. WARNING! The data format in _airbyte_data is likely stable but there are no guarantees that other metadata columns will remain the same in future versions
disable_type_dedupe.title: Disable Final Tables. (WARNING! Unstable option; Columns in raw table schema might change between versions)

drop_cascade.description: Drop tables with CASCADE. WARNING! This will delete all data in all dependent objects (views, etc.). Use with caution. This option is intended for usecases which can easily rebuild the dependent objects.
drop_cascade.title: Drop tables with CASCADE. (WARNING! Risk of unrecoverable data loss)

jdbc_url_params.description: Additional properties to pass to the JDBC URL string when connecting to the database formatted as 'key=value' pairs separated by the symbol '&'. (example: key1=value1&key2=value2&key3=value3).
jdbc_url_params.title: JDBC URL Params

raw_data_schema.description: The schema to write raw tables into
raw_data_schema.title: Raw table schema (defaults to airbyte_internal)

schema.description: The default schema tables are written to if the source does not specify a namespace. The usual value for this field is "public".
schema.title: Default Schema

ssl.description: Encrypt data using SSL. When activating SSL, please select one of the connection modes.
ssl.title: SSL Connection

ssl_mode.description: SSL connection modes. disable - Chose this mode to disable encryption of communication between Airbyte and destination database allow - Chose this mode to enable encryption only when required by the source database prefer - Chose this mode to allow unencrypted connection only if the source database does not support encryption require - Chose this mode to always require encryption. If the source database server does not support encryption, connection will fail verify-ca - Chose this mode to always require encryption and to verify that the source database server has a valid SSL certificate verify-full - This is the most secure mode. Chose this mode to always require encryption and to verify the identity of the source database server See more information - in the docs.

oneOf.description: Disable SSL.
oneOf.title: disable

oneOf.description: Allow SSL mode.
oneOf.title: allow

oneOf.description: Prefer SSL mode.
oneOf.title: prefer

oneOf.description: Require SSL mode.
oneOf.title: require

oneOf.description: Verify-ca SSL mode.

client_key_password.description: Password for keystorage. This field is optional. If you do not add it - the password will be generated automatically.
client_key_password.title: Client key password

oneOf.title: verify-ca

oneOf.description: Verify-full SSL mode.

client_key_password.description: Password for keystorage. This field is optional. If you do not add it - the password will be generated automatically.
client_key_password.title: Client key password

oneOf.title: verify-full

ssl_mode.title: SSL modes

oneOf.description: (recommended) Uploads data to S3 and then uses a COPY to insert the data into Redshift. COPY is recommended for production workloads for better speed and scalability. See AWS docs for more details.

access_key_id.title: Access Key ID

file_name_pattern.title: File Name Pattern

format.description: Format of the data output. See here for more details

compression.description: Whether the output files should be compressed. If compression is selected, the output filename will have an extra extension (GZIP: ".jsonl.gz").
compression.title: Compression

compression.description: Whether the output files should be compressed. If compression is selected, the output filename will have an extra extension (GZIP: ".jsonl.gz").
compression.title: Compression

compression_codec.description: The compression algorithm used to compress data. Default to no compression.
compression_codec.title: Compression Codec

block_size_mb.description: This is the size of a row group being buffered in memory. It limits the memory usage when writing. Larger values will improve the IO when reading, but consume more memory when writing. Default: 128 MB.
block_size_mb.title: Block Size (Row Group Size) (MB)

compression_codec.description: The compression algorithm used to compress data pages.
compression_codec.title: Compression Codec

dictionary_page_size_kb.description: There is one dictionary page per column per row group when dictionary encoding is used. The dictionary page size works like the page size but for dictionary. Default: 1024 KB.
dictionary_page_size_kb.title: Dictionary Page Size (KB)

max_padding_size_mb.description: Maximum size allowed as padding to align row groups. This is also the minimum size of a row group. Default: 8 MB.
max_padding_size_mb.title: Max Padding Size (MB)

page_size_kb.description: The page size is for compression. A block is composed of pages. A page is the smallest unit that must be read fully to access a single record. If this value is too small, the compression will deteriorate. Default: 1024 KB.
page_size_kb.title: Page Size (KB)

format.title: Output Format

schema.description:
The configuration model for the Vector DB based destinations. This model is used to generate the UI for the destination configuration,
as well as to provide type safety for the configuration passed to the destination.

The configuration model is composed of four parts:
* Processing configuration
* Embedding configuration
* Indexing configuration
* Advanced configuration

Processing, embedding and advanced configuration are provided by this base class, while the indexing configuration is provided by the destination connector in the sub class.

oneOf.description: Use the OpenAI API to embed text. This option is using the text-embedding-ada-002 model with 1536 embedding dimensions.
oneOf.title: OpenAI

oneOf.description: Use the Cohere API to embed text.
oneOf.title: Cohere

oneOf.description: Use a fake embedding made out of random vectors with 1536 embedding dimensions. This is useful for testing the data pipeline without incurring any costs.
oneOf.title: Fake

oneOf.description: Use the Azure-hosted OpenAI API to embed text. This option is using the text-embedding-ada-002 model with 1536 embedding dimensions.

deployment.title: Deployment

openai_key.title: Azure OpenAI API key

base_url.title: Base URL

model_name.title: Model name

metadata_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be stored as metadata. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered metadata fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array. When specifying nested paths, all matching values are flattened into an array set to a field named by the path.
metadata_fields.title: Fields to store as metadata

text_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be used to calculate the embedding. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered text fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array.
text_fields.title: Text fields to embed

keep_separator.description: Whether to keep the separator in the resulting chunks
keep_separator.title: Keep separator

separators.description: List of separator strings to split text fields by. The separator itself needs to be wrapped in double quotes, e.g. to split by the dot character, use ".". To split by a newline, use "\n".
separators.title: Separators

oneOf.description: Split the text by Markdown headers down to the specified header level. If the chunk size fits multiple sections, they will be combined into a single chunk.

schema.title: Destination Config

schema.description:
The configuration model for the Vector DB based destinations. This model is used to generate the UI for the destination configuration,
as well as to provide type safety for the configuration passed to the destination.

The configuration model is composed of four parts:
* Processing configuration
* Embedding configuration
* Indexing configuration
* Advanced configuration

Processing, embedding and advanced configuration are provided by this base class, while the indexing configuration is provided by the destination connector in the sub class.

oneOf.description: Do not calculate and pass embeddings to Weaviate. Suitable for clusters with configured vectorizers to calculate embeddings within Weaviate or for classes that should only support regular text search.
oneOf.title: No external embedding

oneOf.description: Use the Azure-hosted OpenAI API to embed text. This option is using the text-embedding-ada-002 model with 1536 embedding dimensions.

api_base.description: The base URL for your Azure OpenAI resource.  You can find this in the Azure portal under your Azure OpenAI resource
api_base.title: Resource base URL

deployment.description: The deployment for your Azure OpenAI resource.  You can find this in the Azure portal under your Azure OpenAI resource
deployment.title: Deployment

openai_key.description: The API key for your Azure OpenAI resource.  You can find this in the Azure portal under your Azure OpenAI resource
openai_key.title: Azure OpenAI API key

oneOf.title: Azure OpenAI

oneOf.description: Use the OpenAI API to embed text. This option is using the text-embedding-ada-002 model with 1536 embedding dimensions.
oneOf.title: OpenAI

oneOf.description: Use the Cohere API to embed text.
oneOf.title: Cohere

oneOf.description: Use a field in the record as the embedding. This is useful if you already have an embedding for your data and want to store it in the vector store.

dimensions.description: The number of dimensions the embedding model is generating
dimensions.title: Embedding dimensions

field_name.description: Name of the field in the record that contains the embedding
field_name.title: Field name

oneOf.title: From Field

oneOf.description: Use a fake embedding made out of random vectors with 1536 embedding dimensions. This is useful for testing the data pipeline without incurring any costs.
oneOf.title: Fake

oneOf.description: Use a service that's compatible with the OpenAI API to embed text.

base_url.description: The base URL for your OpenAI-compatible service
base_url.title: Base URL

dimensions.description: The number of dimensions the embedding model is generating
dimensions.title: Embedding dimensions

model_name.description: The name of the model to use for embedding
model_name.title: Model name

oneOf.title: OpenAI-compatible

additional_headers.description: Additional HTTP headers to send with every request.
additional_headers.title: Additional headers

oneOf.description: Authenticate using an API token (suitable for Weaviate Cloud)

token.description: API Token for the Weaviate instance
token.title: API Token

oneOf.title: API Token

oneOf.description: Authenticate using username and password (suitable for self-managed Weaviate clusters)
oneOf.title: Username/Password

oneOf.description: Do not authenticate (suitable for locally running test clusters, do not use for clusters with public IP addresses)
oneOf.title: No Authentication

batch_size.description: The number of records to send to Weaviate in each batch
batch_size.title: Batch Size

default_vectorizer.description: The vectorizer to use if new classes need to be created
default_vectorizer.title: Default Vectorizer

host.description: The public endpoint of the Weaviate cluster.
host.title: Public Endpoint

tenant_id.description: The tenant ID to use for multi tenancy
tenant_id.title: Tenant ID

text_field.description: The field in the object that contains the embedded text
text_field.title: Text Field

omit_raw_text.description: Do not store the text that gets embedded along with the vector and the metadata in the destination. If set to true, only the vector and the metadata will be stored - in this case raw text for LLM use cases needs to be retrieved from another source.
omit_raw_text.title: Do not store raw text

chunk_overlap.description: Size of overlap between chunks in tokens to store in vector store to better capture relevant context
chunk_overlap.title: Chunk overlap

chunk_size.description: Size of chunks in tokens to store in vector store (make sure it is not too big for the context if your LLM)
chunk_size.title: Chunk size

field_name_mappings.description: List of fields to rename. Not applicable for nested fields, but can be used to rename fields already flattened via dot notation.

from_field.description: The field name in the source
from_field.title: From field name

to_field.description: The field name to use in the destination
to_field.title: To field name

field_name_mappings.title: Field name mappings

metadata_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be stored as metadata. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered metadata fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array. When specifying nested paths, all matching values are flattened into an array set to a field named by the path.
metadata_fields.title: Fields to store as metadata

text_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be used to calculate the embedding. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered text fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array.
text_fields.title: Text fields to embed

text_splitter.description: Split text fields into chunks based on the specified method.

oneOf.description: Split the text by the list of separators until the chunk size is reached, using the earlier mentioned separators where possible. This is useful for splitting text fields by paragraphs, sentences, words, etc.

keep_separator.description: Whether to keep the separator in the resulting chunks
keep_separator.title: Keep separator

separators.description: List of separator strings to split text fields by. The separator itself needs to be wrapped in double quotes, e.g. to split by the dot character, use ".". To split by a newline, use "\n".
separators.title: Separators

oneOf.title: By Separator

oneOf.description: Split the text by Markdown headers down to the specified header level. If the chunk size fits multiple sections, they will be combined into a single chunk.

split_level.description: Level of markdown headers to split text fields by. Headings down to the specified level will be used as split points
split_level.title: Split level

oneOf.title: By Markdown header

oneOf.description: Split the text by suitable delimiters based on the programming language. This is useful for splitting code into chunks.

language.description: Split code in suitable places based on the programming language
language.title: Language

oneOf.title: By Programming Language

text_splitter.title: Text splitter

schema.title: Destination Config
